meta.title: eCTD Cross-Reference Map — Required Inter-Module Links
meta.authority: ICH M4(R4) Organisation of the CTD, Step 4, June 15, 2016; ICH M4Q (Quality), M4S (Safety), M4E (Efficacy)
meta.principle: The CTD is a layered document. Module 2 summarizes Modules 3-5. Every element of data in Modules 3, 4, and 5 MUST have a corresponding summary entry in Module 2. Cross-references ensure reviewers can navigate between the detailed study reports and the integrated summaries without redundant data.

fundamental_rules.rule: Module 2.3 (Quality Overall Summary) MUST summarize ALL quality data in Module 3
fundamental_rules.implication: Every section of Module 3.2.S and 3.2.P has a corresponding section in 2.3.S and 2.3.P. Any rule that maps data to Module 3 MUST also map the summary to the corresponding Module 2.3 sub-section.

fundamental_rules.rule: Module 2.4 (Nonclinical Overview) and Module 2.6 (Nonclinical Written/Tabulated Summaries) MUST summarize ALL nonclinical study reports in Module 4
fundamental_rules.implication: Any rule mapping data to Module 4.2.x MUST also reference Module 2.6 (written summary) and Module 2.4 (integrated overview).

fundamental_rules.rule: Module 2.5 (Clinical Overview) and Module 2.7 (Clinical Summary) MUST summarize ALL clinical study reports in Module 5
fundamental_rules.implication: Any rule mapping data to Module 5.3.x MUST also reference Module 2.7 (sub-section summary) and Module 2.5 (integrated clinical overview).

fundamental_rules.rule: Module 2 summaries cross-reference Module 3-5 for supporting data; Modules 3-5 cross-reference Module 2 for the synthesized interpretation
fundamental_rules.implication: The cross-reference is bidirectional. Automated compliance checking must verify BOTH directions.

Module 3.2.S.7_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 2.3.S.7 (QOS stability summary must match 3.2.S.7 protocol and results)
Module 3.2.S.4 (specifications — shelf life drives retest date in spec)

stability.note: For biologics, stability (Q5C) data goes in same 3.2.S.7/3.2.P.8 locations, with QOS summary in 2.3.S.7/2.3.P.8

Module_3_2_S_4_1_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 3.2.S.7 (stability — limits derived from stability data)
Module 3.2.S.3 (characterization — identifies impurities that appear in specs)

Module_3_2_S_3_2_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 4.2.3.7.6 (if tox study performed for qualification)
Module_4_2_3_7_6_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 3.2.S.4.5 (limit set based on this study)

Module_3_2_S_2_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 3.2.S.4 (control strategy flows from process understanding)

primary_data_modules: Module 3.2.A.2 (Adventitious Agents Safety Evaluation — THE primary location)

Module_3_2_A_2_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 3.2.S.2.3 (cell bank used in manufacture — listed in control of materials)
Module_3_2_S_2_3_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 3.2.A.2 (full viral safety evaluation for cell bank)

summary_modules: Module 2.6.2 (Pharmacology Written Summary — safety pharmacology sub-section)
Module 2.4 (Nonclinical Overview — cardiac safety integrated narrative)

Module_4_2_1_3_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 5.3.4.1 (TQT/clinical cardiac safety — bidirectional link to nonclinical)

bidirectional_clinical_link.nonclinical_to_clinical: Module 4.2.1.3 hERG/QT data → Module 5.3.4.1 (TQT study) — nonclinical findings drive clinical cardiac monitoring design
bidirectional_clinical_link.clinical_to_nonclinical: Module 5.3.4.1 TQT results → inform adequacy of Module 4.2.1.3 nonclinical package; ICH E14/S7B integrated assessment required

label_cross_reference: Module 1 (prescribing information / SmPC — reproductive risk language derived from these studies)

Module_4_2_2_4_metabolism_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 5.3.2.2 (human hepatic metabolism — compare to animal metabolism for species relevance)
Module 5.3.3.4 (clinical DDI — if nonclinical metabolism predicts clinical interaction)

summary_modules: Module 2.7.1 (Summary of Biopharmaceutics Studies and Associated Analytical Methods)

Module_5_3_1_2_BE_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 3.2.P.2 (dissolution and BCS class — basis for biowaiver if applicable)
Module 3.2.P.5.1 (dissolution specification — derived from BE study)
Module_5_3_1_4_bioanalytical_MUST_REFERE: Module 5.3.3 (PK studies that used this method)

Module_5_3_3_3_intrinsic_factors_MUST_RE: Module 2.7.2 (clinical pharm summary — special populations section)
Module_5_3_3_4_drug_interactions_MUST_RE: Module 5.3.2.2 (in vitro DDI from human biomaterials)

Module_5_3_5_3_integrated_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 2.7.3 (the integrated analysis informs the efficacy summary)

regional_modules: Module 1 (PSUR/RMP/PBRER — regional submissions in EU, US, Japan)

drug_interaction_complete.description: Drug interaction data spans nonclinical and clinical domains and must be cross-referenced across three modules
clinical: Module 5.3.3.4 (extrinsic factor PK — DDI clinical studies)
summary: Module 2.6.4 (Nonclinical PK Written Summary — in vitro DDI section)
Module 2.7.2 (Clinical Pharmacology Summary — DDI clinical section)
Module 2.5 (Clinical Overview — DDI impact on dosing)

Module_4_2_2_6_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 5.3.3.4 (does clinical DDI confirm nonclinical prediction?)
Module_5_3_3_4_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 4.2.2.6 (nonclinical basis for clinical DDI study design)

pediatric_complete.description: Pediatric data spans nonclinical, clinical, and administrative modules

Module_4_2_3_5_juvenile_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 5.3.3.3 (pediatric clinical PK — does juvenile animal data predict pediatric clinical findings?)
Module_5_3_3_3_pediatric_PK_MUST_REFEREN: Module 5.3.5.1 (pediatric trials using this PK data)
Module_1_PSP_PIP_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 5.3.5.1 (pediatric study reports fulfilling PSP/PIP commitments)

mutagenic_impurities_complete.description: Mutagenic impurities (ICH M7) span quality characterization, specification justification, and nonclinical genotoxicity
nonclinical: Module 4.2.3.3.1 (Ames assay — in vitro genotoxicity for impurity qualification)
summary: Module 2.6.6 (Toxicology Written Summary — impurity qualification narrative)

Module_3_2_S_3_2_M7_MUST_REFERENCE: Module 4.2.3.3.1 (Ames result if structural alert present)
Module_4_2_3_3_1_impurity_Ames_MUST_REFE: Module 3.2.S.4.5 (limit set based on assay result)

bioequivalence_complete.description: Bioequivalence data spans clinical studies, formulation development, and quality specifications
quality: Module 3.2.P.5.1 (dissolution specification — set based on BE)

Module_3_2_P_2_dissolution_MUST_REFERENC: Module 5.3.1.2 (in vivo BE data supporting dissolution spec)

module_2_to_module_3_5_complete_map.description: Every Module 2 section and its corresponding Module 3/4/5 data sources

module_2_7_REFERENCES_5_3.2.7.6_synopses: → Synopses of individual studies from Module 5.3 (all sub-sections)

automated_validation_rules.description: Machine-executable cross-reference validation rules for automated compliance checking

rules.check: IF rule maps to Module 3.2.S.7 (DS stability) OR Module 3.2.P.8 (DP stability) THEN cross_reference MUST include Module 2.3.S.7 or Module 2.3.P.8 respectively
IF rule maps to Module 4.2.x (any nonclinical study) THEN cross_reference MUST include at minimum Module 2.6.x (corresponding nonclinical summary) AND Module 2.4
IF rule maps to Module 5.3.x (any clinical study) THEN cross_reference MUST include at minimum Module 2.7.x (corresponding clinical summary) AND Module 2.5
IF rule maps to Module 3.2.S.4 (DS control/analytical methods) THEN cross_reference MUST include both Module 3.2.S.4.2 (procedure) AND Module 3.2.S.4.3 (validation) — not just one
IF rule maps to Module 4.2.1.3 (safety pharmacology) THEN cross_reference MUST include Module 5.3.4.1 (TQT — bidirectional E14/S7B link)
IF ectd_module uses 'M' prefix notation (e.g. 'M3.2.P.8') THEN flag as format_error — must use 'Module 3.2.P.8'
IF ectd_module contains Module 1.x sub-sections beyond 1.1 and 1.2 THEN must include jurisdiction qualifier (FDA-specific, EMA-specific, etc.)
IF ectd_module is 'Module 3.2.A' without sub-section (A.1, A.2, or A.3) THEN flag as too_vague
IF rule maps data to Module 3.2.S.3 (characterization) AND rule description mentions 'analytical procedure' or 'method validation' THEN likely_wrong_module — should be Module 3.2.S.4
IF ectd_module contains range notation (e.g. 'M3-M5', 'M1-M5') THEN flag as imprecise — requires specific module enumeration
